machinery_genes.quote: The system is composed of multiple copies of at least thirteen different subunits (TssA‐TssM) and a single copy of the PAAR protein... The role of the TssA subunit is less certain, but it has been proposed to play roles in priming and polymerization of the tube–sheath structure or act as a baseplate component
machinery_genes.note: Spiewak 2019 maps the B. cenocepacia T6SS-1 gene cluster to BCAL0337-BCAL0353 in J2315 and states it encodes the canonical TssA-TssM subunit set; TssA named here as one of those subunits. Locus tag not resolved by me (left for the scripted step).

machinery_genes.quote: The tube, in turn, is surrounded by a contractile sheath composed of polymerized TssBC subunits.
machinery_genes.note: Named as a core subunit (TssA-TssM) of the J2315 T6SS-1 cluster (BCAL0337-BCAL0353) in Spiewak 2019; sheath component.

machinery_genes.quote: The tube, in turn, is surrounded by a contractile sheath composed of polymerized TssBC subunits.
machinery_genes.note: Named as a core subunit (TssA-TssM) of the J2315 T6SS-1 cluster in Spiewak 2019; sheath component.

machinery_genes.role: hexameric inner tube (secreted); also designated TssD
machinery_genes.quote: B. cenocepacia Hcp (BCAL0343) lacks a canonical hydrophobic amino-terminal signal sequence
machinery_genes.note: Aubert 2008 assigns Hcp to BCAL0343 in the J2315 T6SS gene cluster; the atsR mutant hypersecretes this Hcp-like protein in a T6SS-dependent manner. Spiewak 2019: 'multimers of TssD (also termed Hcp) that assemble into a tube'. Machinery here per the brief (secreted tube).

machinery_genes.quote: The tube–sheath structure is assembled on a platform known as the baseplate that consists of the TssEFGK subunits
machinery_genes.note: Named as a core subunit (TssA-TssM) of the J2315 T6SS-1 cluster in Spiewak 2019; baseplate component.

machinery_genes.quote: The tube–sheath structure is assembled on a platform known as the baseplate that consists of the TssEFGK subunits
machinery_genes.note: Named as a core subunit (TssA-TssM) of the J2315 T6SS-1 cluster in Spiewak 2019; baseplate component.

machinery_genes.quote: The tube–sheath structure is assembled on a platform known as the baseplate that consists of the TssEFGK subunits
machinery_genes.note: Named as a core subunit (TssA-TssM) of the J2315 T6SS-1 cluster in Spiewak 2019; baseplate component.

machinery_genes.role: AAA+ ATPase that recycles the contracted sheath; also designated TssH
machinery_genes.quote: the amino acid sequences of TssH (BCAL0347) and TagX proteins (BCAL0352) from J2315 were used as search queries
machinery_genes.note: TssH = ClpV. Spiewak 2019 assigns TssH to BCAL0347 in J2315; Aubert 2008 independently names the same gene 'clpV (BCAL0347; bcsF)' as a characteristic T6SS component. Essential sheath-recycling ATPase, counted per the brief.

machinery_genes.role: trimeric spike at tube tip (secreted); also designated TssI
machinery_genes.quote: a trimer of TssI (also known as VgrG) subunits capped by a monomer of the PAAR protein
machinery_genes.note: Core subunit of the system. Spiewak 2019 notes that in most Bcc species VgrG (TssI) is not in the main T6SS-1 cluster but present in multiple copies at other loci: 'genes encoding the core T6SS subunits TssI and PAAR were not observed to be located in the T6SS-1 gene cluster and are instead present in multiple copies at other loci distributed throughout the genome (as observed for [B. cenocepacia] by Aubert et al.)'. Aubert 2015 systematically deleted ten vgrG genes in K56-2. Counted as machinery (secreted spike) per the brief; no single locus tag asserted.

machinery_genes.quote: The tube–sheath structure is assembled on a platform known as the baseplate that consists of the TssEFGK subunits
machinery_genes.note: Named as a core subunit (TssA-TssM) of the J2315 T6SS-1 cluster in Spiewak 2019; baseplate-membrane linker.

machinery_genes.role: inner-membrane component of the membrane complex (DotU/TssL)
machinery_genes.quote: The second subassembly, composed of the TssJLM subunits, is a channel/chamber complex that spans the inner membrane, periplasm, and outer membrane
machinery_genes.note: Named as a core subunit (TssA-TssM) of the J2315 T6SS-1 cluster in Spiewak 2019; inner-membrane component of the membrane complex (partners IcmF/TssM).

machinery_genes.role: large inner-membrane component of the membrane complex; also designated TssM
machinery_genes.quote: icmF (BCAL0351; bcsB), clpV (BCAL0347; bcsF), and hcp (BCAL0343; bcsJ), which are characteristic of T6SSs in other bacteria
machinery_genes.note: IcmF = TssM. Aubert 2008 (Fig 5) assigns icmF to BCAL0351 in the J2315 T6SS cluster; Spiewak 2019 names TssM as a core subunit and notes IS-element insertion into the tssM gene truncates the TssM subunit. Inner-membrane channel/chamber complex.

machinery_genes.role: spike-sharpening tip protein capping the VgrG spike (secreted)
machinery_genes.quote: a trimer of TssI (also known as VgrG) subunits capped by a monomer of the PAAR protein
machinery_genes.note: Core single-copy component of the system. Spiewak 2019 notes that, like TssI/VgrG, PAAR-domain genes in Bcc are generally not in the main T6SS-1 cluster but distributed at other loci. Counted as machinery (secreted spike tip) per the brief; no single locus tag asserted.

machinery_genes.role: membrane-anchored peptidoglycan hydrolase enabling T6SS sheath assembly through the PG layer
machinery_genes.quote: the amino acid sequences of TssH (BCAL0347) and TagX proteins (BCAL0352) from J2315 were used as search queries
machinery_genes.note: Spiewak 2019 assigns TagX to BCAL0352 in J2315 and describes it as 'a Sec-dependent membrane-anchored peptidoglycan hydrolase that facilitates T6SS sheath assembly through formation of holes in the peptidoglycan layer'. Accessory apparatus component (analogous to TagX/TssM-associated PG remodelling), not a delivered effector.

notes: System: the B. cenocepacia chromosome-1 T6SS-1, the only T6SS of the ET12 epidemic lineage characterized in detail. Spiewak et al. 2019 (MicrobiologyOpen) maps this cluster to BCAL0337-BCAL0353 in the J2315 reference genome (NC_011000.1) and states it encodes the canonical TssA-TssM subunit set plus accessory genes; it is the apparatus that serves the curated effector context for this instance (BCAL0824, a distributed effector). Strain transfer: much of the functional/genetic work (Hcp hypersecretion, systematic gene deletion) used the clonally related ET12 strain K56-2 (Aubert 2008 Infect Immun; Aubert 2015 Microbiology); J2315 and K56-2 are members of the same epidemic lineage and share this T6SS-1 cluster, so locus assignments map across. Locus-anchored, paper-named components: Hcp/TssD = BCAL0343, ClpV/TssH = BCAL0347, IcmF/TssM = BCAL0351, TagX = BCAL0352 (all named in Aubert 2008 Fig 5 and/or Spiewak 2019 methods). Remaining core subunits (TssA, TssB, TssC, TssE, TssF, TssG, TssK, TssL) are listed on the strength of Spiewak's statement that the J2315 T6SS-1 cluster encodes the full TssA-TssM set, with per-subassembly naming quotes; individual BCAL locus tags within BCAL0337-BCAL0353 were deliberately NOT resolved here (coordinate/locus resolution is a separate scripted step and mining the GenBank is disallowed). VgrG (TssI) and PAAR are counted as machinery per the brief (secreted spike/tip) even though Spiewak notes that in Bcc these two are generally encoded outside the main cluster in multiple distributed copies (Aubert 2015 deleted ten vgrG genes in K56-2); no single locus tag asserted for them. TssJ deliberately EXCLUDED: Spiewak reports the genus discussed there lacks a TssJ orthologue (the membrane complex is TssLM-only), and the redacted genus name made it ambiguous whether this applies to Burkholderia, so to avoid hallucination I did not list a TssJ that I cannot defend as present. Deliberately excluded as non-machinery: delivered cargo effectors and immunity proteins (Tle/Tae/Tde and the many BCAL/BCAM effector loci catalogued by Spiewak, e.g. BCAL0824, BCAL1166, BCAL1292, BCAL2505, BCAM1464/1465), the regulator AtsR (BCAM0379, a sensor-kinase/response-regulator that controls but is not part of the apparatus), and TagY (a candidate post-translational regulator). Low-confidence calls: the eight non-locus-anchored core subunits rely on the cluster-level 'encodes TssA-TssM' statement rather than an individual gene-naming sentence; TagX family label assigned from the apparatus-brief accessory vocabulary (paper calls it a Sec-dependent PG hydrolase / undescribed gene).
